← Developer Tools

Color Picker

Pick any color and instantly get its HEX, RGB and HSL values. Copy any format in one click.

Selected color

#0071E3

HEX#0071E3
RGBrgb(0, 113, 227)
HSLhsl(210, 100%, 45%)

How to use the color picker

Click the color swatch to open the native browser color selector. Choose your color by clicking, dragging or entering a hex value directly. The HEX, RGB and HSL codes update instantly. Click Copy next to any format to copy it to your clipboard, ready to paste into your code or design tool.

What is HEX color?

HEX (hexadecimal) color is the most widely used color format in web design and CSS. It is a six-character code preceded by a hash symbol (e.g. #0071e3), where pairs of characters represent the red, green and blue channels on a scale from 00 (zero) to FF (255). HEX is supported everywhere — HTML, CSS, SVG, most design applications and most email clients.

What is RGB color?

RGB stands for Red, Green, Blue. Each channel is expressed as a number from 0 to 255 (e.g. rgb(0, 113, 227)). RGB is the native color model of screens and is used in CSS, image editors and design applications. It is especially useful when you need to set color opacity using rgba() notation, adding a fourth value from 0 (transparent) to 1 (fully opaque).

What is HSL color?

HSL stands for Hue, Saturation, Lightness. Hue is the color angle on a 360-degree wheel (0 = red, 120 = green, 240 = blue). Saturation is the intensity (0% = grey, 100% = full color). Lightness ranges from 0% (black) to 100% (white), with 50% being the pure color. HSL is intuitive for designers because adjusting lightness or saturation while keeping the same hue is much easier than tweaking RGB values manually.

Is this tool free?

Yes. Completely free, no login required. The color picker uses your browser's built-in color selector API — nothing is uploaded or stored.
